public_links.title: Wikipedia: Bicycle Generator Principles and History
Appropedia: Open-Source Pedal Power Generator Engineering Design

technical_documents.description: Converts human pedal input into stable AC/DC output via a rewired alternator and capacitor bank, enabling off-grid device charging from kinetic effort.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Off-grid health clinics cannot refrigerate vaccines or charge diagnostic devices overnight, leading to 30% medicine spoilage in tropical regions.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: A bicycle flywheel drives a rewound car alternator through a 1:6 gear ratio, feeding a bridge rectifier and supercapacitor bank that delivers regulated 12V DC and 110V AC via a pure-sine inverter.

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Strip the bicycle of rear wheel; mount alternator on a steel bracket at chain-stay position.
Step 2: Attach a 12 kg flywheel to the crank axle to smooth pedalling cadence fluctuations.
Step 3: Wire alternator output through bridge rectifier to supercapacitor bank (3 x 100F 2.7V series).
Step 4: Connect capacitor output to pure-sine inverter input terminals.
Step 5: Install a voltmeter display on handlebars; test with a 60W load for 30-minute sustained output.

comments.text: Built for our rural clinic in Uganda. 20 minutes pedalling charges our vaccine fridge for 6 hours.
